login.dataDisclaimer: We do not have access to your password, email, game library, or any other private data. Authentication is handled directly by Steam.

rules.title: QuakeClub Rules
rules.subtitle: Rules for servers and official tournaments
rules.intro: These rules apply to all QuakeClub servers, Discord, and official events. The goal is to maintain a competitive but healthy environment. If you see someone breaking the rules, report them on Discord with evidence (screenshot, demo).
rules.sanctionsNote: Sanctions are cumulative. Admins may skip levels depending on severity.
rules.lastUpdated: Last updated: January 2025. These rules may change without notice.
rules.generalRule1Desc: Treat everyone with respect. Insults, discrimination, and toxicity will not be tolerated. Moderate trash talk is part of the game, but there are limits.
rules.generalRule2Desc: Use of aimbots, wallhacks, speedhacks, or any gameplay-modifying software is prohibited. This includes scripts that automate actions (bunny hop scripts, etc.). Permanent ban.
rules.generalRule3Desc: Do not use alternate accounts to manipulate your ELO or play against lower-level players. Your main Steam ID is your only valid account.
rules.generalRule4Desc: Player and clan names must be appropriate. Nothing offensive, discriminatory, or impersonating another known player.
rules.duelRule1Desc: In ranked matches, spectators have automatic delay. Do not use Discord or other means to provide real-time information to a player.
rules.duelRule2Desc: You can use custom configs (FOV, crosshair, binds). What you CANNOT: extreme picmip that gives visual advantage, auto-hop scripts, model modifications.
rules.duelRule3Desc: Duel matches are 10 minutes. In case of a tie, overtime until someone has a 2-frag advantage or the extra time runs out.
rules.duelRule4Desc: You can pause the match for real emergencies. Abusing pauses to distract the opponent is sanctionable.
rules.caRule1Desc: Although teamkill is disabled in CA, trolling your team (blocking, not playing, revealing positions) is sanctionable.
rules.caRule2Desc: CA is a team mode. Coordinate with your team, don't go solo against everyone or abandon active rounds.
rules.caRule3Desc: Respect the server's autobalance. Don't switch teams to join the winning side.
rules.caRule4Desc: You can play with friends, but if the server has autobalance, accept it. Don't break matches because you didn't end up with your squad.
rules.compRule1Desc: Show up on time for your matches. Being late may result in forfeit according to the specific tournament rules.
rules.compRule2Desc: Play on the server assigned by admins. Don't ask to change because you have better ping on another.
rules.compRule3Desc: In official tournaments, you must record demos of your matches. The command is /record filename. Demos can be requested for review.
rules.compRule4Desc: If there are technical issues or disputes, contact an admin immediately. Don't resolve conflicts by arguing on the server.
rules.compRule5Desc: Admins have the final say in disputes. Accepting decisions is mandatory to participate in future events.
rules.sanction1Desc: First minor offense. Recorded on your profile.
rules.sanction2Desc: 24-72 hours without chat access on servers.
rules.sanction3Desc: 7-30 days without access to QuakeClub servers.
rules.sanction4Desc: Cheats, extreme toxicity, or repeat offenses. No appeal.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1Intro: When using QuakeClub, we collect the following information through Steam authentication:
privacy.section1CountryDesc: Your country set on Steam (if public)
privacy.section1Extra: Additionally, we record data from your matches on our servers such as statistics, scores, and ELO ratings.
privacy.section2Item1: Identify you on the platform and game servers
privacy.section2Item2: Calculate and display your statistics and rankings
privacy.section2Item3: Manage the ELO and matchmaking system
privacy.section2Item5: Moderate the community and apply sanctions if necessary
privacy.section3Text: Your profile information and statistics are public by default and visible to other community users. We do not sell or share your information with third parties outside the platform.
privacy.section4Text: We use technical cookies necessary to keep your session active and remember your preferences (such as selected language). We do not use advertising tracking cookies.
privacy.section5Text: We implement security measures to protect your information, including encrypted connections (HTTPS) and secure authentication through Steam. However, no system is completely secure.
privacy.section6Text: We may update this policy occasionally. Significant changes will be announced on our Discord or in the news section.
privacy.section7Text: If you have questions about this policy, contact us through our Discord server.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.section1Text: By accessing and using QuakeClub (quakeclub.com), you agree to comply with these terms of service. If you disagree with any of these terms, please do not use our services.
terms.section2Intro: QuakeClub is a community platform that offers:
terms.section2Item1: Quake Live ranking and statistics system with real-time ELO
terms.section2Item2: Player profiles with match history and detailed statistics
terms.section2Item4: Resources like configurations and HUDs shared by the community
terms.section3Intro: To access certain features, you must authenticate through Steam. By doing so:
terms.section3Item1: You guarantee that the Steam account belongs to you
terms.section3Item2: You accept that your public profile information will be visible on the platform
terms.section3Item3: You agree not to share your session with third parties
terms.section3Item4: You are responsible for all actions performed from your account
terms.section4Intro: By using QuakeClub, you agree to:
terms.section4Item2: Not use hacks, cheats, or unauthorized software
terms.section4Item3: Not perform spam, phishing, or any malicious activity
terms.section4Item4: Not publish offensive, illegal, or inappropriate content
terms.section4Item5: Comply with specific rules of each server and tournament
terms.section5Intro: Violation of these terms may result in:
terms.section5Note: Moderation decisions are made by the administration team and can be appealed through our official channels.
terms.section6Text1: All original QuakeClub content, including design, code, and texts, is property of QuakeClub. Quake and Quake Live are registered trademarks of id Software/Bethesda.
terms.section6Text2: User-generated content (configurations, HUDs) remains the property of their respective authors, who grant QuakeClub a license to distribute it on the platform.
terms.section7Text: QuakeClub may display third-party ads to fund the platform's operation. We commit to non-invasive advertising that respects the user experience.
terms.section8Intro: QuakeClub is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We are not responsible for:
terms.section8Item4: Damages derived from using the platform
terms.section9Text: We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Changes will be communicated through our official channels. Continued use of the platform implies acceptance of the new terms.
terms.section10Text: For questions about these terms, you can contact us through Discord:

about.title: About Us
about.intro: {quakeClub} is the competitive Quake Live community in Chile, whose primary goal is to bring game enthusiasts together around the national competitive scene. Through organizing events and tournaments, both online and in-person, we seek to actively contribute to local development, integrating experienced players and boosting the level of new competitors in strategic shooters.
about.teamIntro: Our team consists of four passionate members: {forest}, {hexen}, {pez}, and {geze}. As an organization, we strive to maintain a close and friendly style, where harmony between competition and personal relationships prevails, creating an environment where each player feels supported and part of our identity.
about.infraIntro: We differentiate ourselves by offering technical tools developed by players for players:
about.infraDataDesc: We have created an original monitoring and statistics tracking system that updates player performance and ELO rankings in real time. This allows players to level up their game and compare themselves with the rest of the community.
about.infraServersDesc: We have competitive and public servers in Chile and Brazil, allowing us to expand our network and integrate players from Argentina, Peru, Bolivia, and other Southern Cone countries.
about.infraDigitalDesc: We maintain a highly active community on Discord, we livestream our competitions via Twitch, and we have a historical archive on YouTube to relive the most iconic moments of our journey.
about.closing: We recognize that {quake} is a classic game that laid the foundations of esports worldwide. Although it currently maintains an active community, mainly composed of players between 25 and 40 years old with more activity at night, our goal is to reach new generations. We want to attract players from other titles to discover Quake Live, thereby increasing the volume of the scene and ensuring that the platform remains a faithful and quality support over time.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: We are here to help
contact.intro: If you have questions, suggestions, or need help with any aspect of QuakeClub, don't hesitate to contact us. Our team is committed to responding as soon as possible.
contact.teamDesc: QuakeClub is managed by a team of passionate players dedicated to keeping the Quake Live community alive in Latin America.
